{"query":"digital euro European Parliament vote first reading result status cash replacement confirmation 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1557,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.europarl.europa.eu/news/en/press-room/20260622IPR45912/digital-euro-meps-want-to-ensure-sovereignty-privacy-and-financial-stability","title":"Digital euro: MEPs want to ensure sovereignty, privacy and financial stability | News | European Parliament","content":"Jun 23, 2026 — The one on the establishment of the digital euro was adopted by 43 votes to 14, with 1 abstention. adopted by 43 votes to 9, with 6 abstentions,
The digital euro will complement cash, never replace it."},{"url":"https://www.globalgovernmentfinance.com/digital-euro-parliament-econ-committee-vote/","title":"Digital euro moves closer to reality as ECON committee votes ‘oui’","content":"The digital euro has moved a step closer to reality, with the European Parliament (EP)’s economic and monetary affairs committee having given a green light to the eurozone central bank digital currency (CBDC).
The parliamentary committee adopted its position on the single currency package, which comprises three files, on 23 June.
Under an assumption that Europe’s co-legislators adopt a regulation on the establishment of the digital euro this year (2026), pilot activity and ‘initial transactions’ remain slated to take place as of ‘mid-2027’.
The Eurosystem – which comprises the ECB and the national central banks of the 21 European Union (EU) member states whose currency is the euro – ‘should then be ready for a potential first issuance of the digital euro during 2029,’ the central bank said last October.
Member states would also need to check cash availability ‘regularly’, ceding ‘special attention’ to vulnerable groups, such as the elderly, low-income individuals and the unbanked.
RELATED ARTICLE ECB preps for digital euro launch in 2029 – a news story (4 November 2025) on the ECB Governing Council giving the nod to move to the latest phase of its digital euro preparations
“With the single currency package, we are protecting citizens’ freedom to choose how they pay,” said Spanish MEP and rapporteur (lead lawmaker steering the legislation) Fernando Navarrete Rojas.
This will allow European payment solutions to connect to a common acceptance infrastructure and become interoperable across borders,” he said."},{"url":"https://www.centralbanking.com/fintech/cbdc/7976215/digital-euro-passes-european-parliaments-first-hurdle","title":"Digital euro passes European Parliament’s first hurdle - Central Banking","content":"The committee approved the “establishment of the digital euro” with 43 votes in favour, 14 against and one abstention within the “single currency package”, it announced in a press release today (June 23).
Central Banking understands the legislation will now move to the parliament’s plenary on the week of July 6."},{"url":"https://www.rfi.fr/en/international/20260624-digital-euro-wins-key-eu-backing-as-lawmakers-approve-draft-rules","title":"Digital euro wins key EU backing as lawmakers approve draft rules - RFI","content":"The European Union has moved a step closer to creating a digital euro, after members of the European Parliament passed a crucial vote on the virtual currency.
The European Central Bank (ECB) secured key parliamentary backing on Tuesday for the launch of the digital euro – an electronic currency aimed at making the eurozone less reliant on American-owned payment systems such as Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay and Google Pay.
The ECB first suggested the digital euro in 2020, before the European Commission – the EU's executive branch – made its formal proposal.
“It wouldn't replace anything,” said Giovannini.
Supporters say the digital currency is key to Europe’s ability to control its financial infrastructure."},{"url":"https://howtheyvote.eu/votes/195719","title":"Establishment of the digital euro · Vote Results · HowTheyVote.eu","content":"Press releases often contain more information about the subject of the vote and next steps.
Find out more about the procedure this vote is part of."}]}

The digital euro would offer citizens and businesses a private, secure and innovative way to pay, while reducing reliance on non-EU providers. The one on the establishment of the digital euro) was adopted by 43 votes to 14, with 1 abstention. The digital euro would be a new, electronic form of money issued by the European Central Bank (ECB) and would work online and offline. Privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles would be built into the digital euro. A second file on the provision of digital euro services by payment services providers incorporated in member states whose currency is not the euro), adopted by 43 votes to 9, with 6 abstentions, would allow banks and PSPs from non-euro EU countries to distribute the digital euro, subject to the same rules, while the ECB would retain the power to restrict access and use.","score":0.45711306,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/deutschewellenews/posts/the-digital-euro-is-one-step-closer-to-reality-after-winning-a-key-vote-in-the-e/1479722037516439","title":"The digital euro is one step closer to reality after winning ...","content":"The digital euro is one step closer to reality after winning a key vote in the European Parliament. You would be able to use a digital euro to pay for goods and services in any euro area business that already accepts a digital means of payment (such as, credit or debit card payments).
